panels.title: CI checks per minute, by class
panels.description: Counts ci_done events grouped by classifier verdict — `success` (real green), `target_fail` (a tier package broke, ci-check still exits 0), `infra_error` (everything else: realm prep, freeze, network).

panels.title: Top failing packages (last 1h)
panels.description: Aggregates `node failed: ... <pkg>` markers across every ci-check run in the window. Persistent culprits dominate; flapping nodes show up as smaller bars.

panels.title: Per-tier success ratio
panels.description: success / (success + target_fail + infra_error) over a 10-min window, grouped by tier. Drops below 1.0 mean either a real package broke or our infra is flaking.

targets.expr: sum by (tier) (count_over_time({service="ci_metrics"} | json | class="success" [10m])) / sum by (tier) (count_over_time({service="ci_metrics"} | json | tier!="" [10m]))

panels.title: Check duration p50 / p95 (s)
panels.description: Two clusters expected: ~17s tasks (cache hit, no real build) and longer ones when a tier actually compiles something. p95 spike means the long tail moved.

panels.title: Recent infra errors
panels.description: ci-check runs the classifier marked `infra_error` — neither real success nor a recognised target failure. These are the ones to triage: realm prep crashes, freeze ImportErrors, network blips. Click through to Loki Explore to read the full stderr.

targets.expr: {service="ci_metrics"} | json | class="infra_error" | line_format "{{.tier}}  host={{.host}}  failed_node={{.failed_node}}  dur={{.dur}}s  guid={{.guid}}"
